history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed on JDK 11 due to missing javax.xml.bind.JAXBException, which was removed from the JDK starting in Java 11.
history.qwen_rationale: Adding explicit JAXB API and runtime dependencies restores the javax.xml.bind classes required by the codebase during the Java 11 compilation step, allowing the migration to proceed.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed during the Java 11 compilation step with `NoClassDefFoundError: javax/xml/bind/JAXBException`, indicating that the `maven-compiler-plugin` (v3.8.0) or an annotation processor cannot resolve the JAXB API on the JDK 11 classpath despite the dependency addition.
history.qwen_rationale: Upgrading `maven-compiler-plugin` to 3.11.0 resolves known classpath isolation and JDK 11+ compatibility issues that frequently cause JAXB-related compilation failures. Explicitly adding `javax.activation-api` alongside the JAXB runtime ensures all required `javax.xml.bind` transitive dependencies are available on the compile classpath.

history.qwen_observation: Build failed at JDK 11 compilation due to NoClassDefFoundError for javax/xml/bind/JAXBException, indicating the JAXB API was added but the runtime implementation was missing from the compile classpath, compounded by potential classpath resolution quirks in maven-compiler-plugin 3.11.0.
history.qwen_rationale: Adding org.glassfish.jaxb:jaxb-runtime alongside the API ensures all JAXB classes are available during compilation. Switching to UpgradeToJava11 provides more robust property synchronization, and downgrading the compiler plugin to 3.10.1 avoids known classpath isolation issues with older projects on newer plugin versions.
